address: Streat Barn Cottage, Streat, Sussex BN6 8RX
lead_profile_text: 4-bedroom bungalow adjacent to Natalia's own home. Fully furnished, freshly renovated with garden and roof terrace. Previously self-managed as long-let at £3,400/month gross; tenants left ~2 months ago. Motivations: bad tenants, Section 21 concerns, potential future sale, and desire for flexibility and control. Follow-up from entry 041.
emotional_profile: Split between income and control. Financial uplift broadly as expected — not a dramatic surprise. Control, flexibility, and Section 21 protection are at least as important as income maximisation. Primary gate is decision timing (1–2 weeks).
green_light_trigger_phrases: Target launch mid-July to capture August bookings

top_objections.text: Revenue is roughly what she expected — implicit question of whether switching is worth it vs existing long-let income of £3,400/month gross
top_objections.zac_response: Pitch shifted to flexibility, control, and Section 21 protection narrative — not income maximisation. STL nets £1,566/month more than managed long-let equivalent.

top_objections.text: Property is on oil (not gas); EPCR due for renewal soon — compliance logistics concern
top_objections.zac_response: To be addressed in onboarding. Oil heating follows a different EPC compliance path — not a blocker but needs planning.

top_objections.text: No Slack experience — unfamiliar with the communication channel
top_objections.zac_response: Framed by Zac as 'business WhatsApp' — Natalia immediately comfortable with this description.

top_questions.text: How does Stayful handle linen, supplies, and day-to-day operations?
top_questions.answer: Stayful manages linen hire, consumable supplies, and all day-to-day guest operations as part of full management.

top_questions.text: Can I sell the property while it is listed on short-let?
top_questions.answer: Yes — no fixed long-term tenant commitment. You retain full flexibility to sell with standard notice period.

what_moved_them: Flexibility vs long-let tenancy — no fixed term, can sell with notice at any time
Stayful managing linen hire, supplies, and all day-to-day operations
Target launch mid-July to capture August peak bookings
13%+VAT special offer framed as a time-limited opportunity
what_fell_flat: Revenue broadly as expected — no financial surprise to anchor conversion on income
Income uplift (£1,566/month) less dramatic from her perspective given she was already grossing £3,400/month self-managed

conversion_gaps.gap: Financial uplift is not the conversion lever — control and flexibility are
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Lead follow-up on Section 21 protection, sell-readiness, and flexibility narrative rather than income delta.

conversion_gaps.gap: Oil heating EPCR compliance logistics not resolved
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Confirm oil EPC compliance path during onboarding; ensure renewal timeline does not delay mid-July launch.

offer_framing: 13%+VAT expires 25 June. Mid-July launch anchored to August peak season capture. Control and flexibility framing dominant over income maximisation.
zac_close_phrase: Aim for mid-July launch to capture the full August peak — two weeks to set up once you confirm go-ahead
zac_income_intro: ~£3,137/month net at full run rate (£37,647/year). Low months (Nov/Jan) ~£2,200–2,500 net; peak (Aug) ~£4,400. STL nets £1,566/month more than the equivalent managed long-let comparison.
zac_transition_phrases: Business WhatsApp — you manage everything from one place on your phone
zac_reassurance_phrases: No fixed-term commitment — you can sell at any time with proper notice
Section 21 changes make long-let tenants much harder to move on — STL gives you complete control
ltl_monthly: £3,400 gross self-managed previously (~£1,571 managed net equivalent used for comparison)
